title: Yui absent from the axis of evil
display_title: Yui absent from the axis of evil
wikitext:
[]: Yui absent from the axis of evil?             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Jun 09, 2007 3:56 am Why is it that when Fuyutski is recruited, the scene is him, eva-00 protoype, Gendou, and Naoko Akagi? Yui was as involved as any of them. Sure, she had an errand.. or something. But really, what were the creators trying to say by leaving her out of the critical "Join us!" scene?

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sat Jun 09, 2007 4:24 am Yui (as a human at least) isn't ominous enough, probably.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sat Jun 09, 2007 9:10 am I think you're overanalysing things.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jun 09, 2007 9:26 am Ironfoot wrote: I think you're overanalysing things.

[]:             Quote Post by Bean Bandit » Sat Jun 09, 2007 10:04 am She was clearly on a coffee break, nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Jun 09, 2007 10:36 am clearly, when you're on a coffee break, you leave the building with a clipboard instead of say.. getting coffee from the breakroom. Ironfoot wrote: I think you're overanalysing things.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sat Jun 09, 2007 10:39 am Well I guess it does seem weird that she doesn't appear...

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Jun 09, 2007 11:38 am Sorry if my first post didn't explain my thoughts well enough, I was teh drunk! Executor wrote: As do I *pulls out Benelli M3shotty* Die, bitch.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sat Jun 09, 2007 2:50 pm slothen wrote: <snip>

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Jun 09, 2007 4:13 pm Opteron-O3 wrote: slothen wrote: <snip>

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 09, 2007 5:35 pm I guess they just felt Yui was too tied up with her mission to bother showing that? *shrugs* (Topic, bitches 'n bastards, topic!)

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jun 09, 2007 7:28 pm Ironfoot wrote: I think you're overanalysing things.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sat Jun 09, 2007 8:41 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Ironfoot wrote: I think you're overanalysing things.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Jun 10, 2007 12:59 am Why does anyone think there is some hidden message in this? I think it's rather clear what she was doing while this was going on. She was leaving as Fuyutsuki got there and later had a chat with him with Shinji in tow, so obviously she went to pick Shinji up.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jun 10, 2007 9:14 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Why does anyone think there is some hidden message in this? I think it's rather clear what she was doing while this was going on. She was leaving as Fuyutsuki got there and later had a chat with him with Shinji in tow, so obviously she went to pick Shinji up.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jun 10, 2007 10:25 am when I say "axis of evil" I certainly mean it in a facetious way.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 10, 2007 11:39 am Apart from the dive suit image -- present only in one version of the story -- we never see Yui in the GeoFront. Most of the time we see her outdoors. I wonder if there is some sort of Earth Mother symbolism, however unintentional.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jun 10, 2007 11:57 am She's closely associated with the ideas of hope*, rebirth, and the survival of mankind. Its ironic that the single champion of hope would be "dead" for the entire series. *which suddenly makes me think of that wierd "Pandora's box" conversation Seele and Kaworu have in 24'

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:14 pm From what I've seen, I always thought of Yui to be that perfect mother figure. Sure we didn't seen much of her but from the scenes that were shown, I got a sense that she just loved Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 10, 2007 3:00 pm Yui even said in the 21' discussion with Fuyutsuki and the end of it which we see in EoE that she's doing what she is for Shinji's sake, so I agree. ;)
